intro: Mycelis glandulosa, commonly known as wild valerian or spotted wintergreen, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Saxifragaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief from stress, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as alleviating symptoms of menopause and menstrual cramps. The bioactive constituents of Mycelis glandulosa include valerenic acid, valepotriates, and isovaleric acid, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
